scope.goal: Implement and prove the Orders source-owned PostgreSQL outbox and Wolverine-backed relay/delivery boundary.
scope.target: Orders atomic commit/outbox boundary and shared integration-event delivery ports
constraints: Do not close MSG-001 without atomic failure-window evidence
Automated messaging tests use local/in-memory transport
Real Kafka/RabbitMQ connectivity is manual opt-in

user_constraints: Address MUST FIX findings after critical direction

evidence: Order event-store commit precedes publisher call
Durable endpoints lack configured message persistence
acceptance: Project-owned port and transaction owner are explicit
Commit-to-enqueue failure window is proven closed or finding remains open

steps: Define source transaction and transport-neutral delivery ports
Write Order events and source-outbox rows in one PostgreSQL transaction
Relay stable message identities through Wolverine PostgreSQL persistence
Add broker-free local-transport tests and PostgreSQL failure-injection tests
Record relay duplicate and recovery behavior
validation: Required PostgreSQL transaction rollback/commit and relay recovery tests
Real Kafka/RabbitMQ connectivity only by explicit manual command
execution.commit_checkpoint: Commit the bounded durable messaging slice

results.summary: Implemented a Dapper source outbox that writes OrderEvents and OrderIntegrationOutbox in one transaction, then relays a stable source message identity through Wolverine PostgreSQL persistence. Added existing-volume migration, owner-token claims, per-row retry/backoff, parking, and broker-free retry identity tests. Database-backed failure-injection and multi-worker recovery tests remain before MSG-001 can close.
validation_evidence: Orders WebApi and full solution builds passed
Code-level transaction boundary writes domain and source-outbox rows before one commit
Retry tests preserve source row Id as Wolverine deduplication metadata and partition key
